title: Infosys Board Approves ₹ 18,000 Crore Equity Share Buyback
detailed_summary: Infosys Limited’s Board of Directors approved a proposal to buy back up to 10,00,00,000 fully paid-up equity shares of face value ₹ 5/- each for an aggregate amount of ₹ 18,000 Crore at ₹ 1,800/- per Equity Share, payable in cash. The proposed buyback represents up to 2.41% of the total number of Equity Shares in the existing paid-up equity share capital on a standalone basis and will be conducted through the tender offer route on a proportionate basis from shareholders as on a record date to be announced later. The company noted that ADS holders may participate if they cancel ADSs and withdraw underlying equity shares by the Record Date. The buyback remains subject to shareholder approval through remote e-voting and applicable statutory approvals, and Infosys will seek SEC exemptive relief due to conflicting Indian and U.S. tender offer requirements.

key_facts.fact: The Board approved a proposal to buy back equity shares for an amount of ₹ 18,000 Crore at a price of ₹ 1,800/- per Equity Share, payable in cash.

The proposed buyback comprises purchase of 10,00,00,000 fully paid-up equity shares of face value ₹ 5/- each, representing up to 2.41% of the total number of Equity Shares in the existing total paid-up Equity Share capital of the Company on a standalone basis.

The buyback is proposed from all equity shareholders as on a Record Date to be announced later, including ADS holders who cancel ADSs and withdraw underlying equity shares to become equity shareholders as on the Record Date.
The buyback is proposed through the tender offer route on a proportionate basis in accordance with the SEBI Buyback Regulations and the Companies Act, 2013.

The Buyback Size does not exceed 25% of the aggregate of the paid-up capital and free reserves, based on the latest audited interim condensed standalone and consolidated financial statements as on June 30, 2025.
The buyback has not yet been approved by the Company’s shareholders and has not yet commenced.
key_facts.section_heading: Additional information pursuant to U.S. Law

key_facts.fact: Infosys will seek exemptive relief from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on certain aspects of the tender offer procedures due to conflicting regulatory requirements between Indian and U.S. laws.

corporate_actions.description: Infosys Board approved a proposal to buy back up to 10,00,00,000 fully paid-up equity shares of face value ₹ 5/- each through the tender offer route on a proportionate basis from shareholders as on a Record Date to be announced later.

risk_factors.risk: The buyback has not yet been approved by shareholders and has not yet commenced.
risk_factors.potential_impact: Offers to purchase or solicitations of offers to sell will be made only if the Buyback is approved by shareholders.

processing_notes: Pre-buyback shareholding pattern was not fully extracted because the main investor-relevant event is the buyback; only material buyback scale, terms, approvals, ADS participation context, and key regulatory dependency were retained.
